description: Convert user starter resumes from data/starter_resumes into strict BaseResumeSchema JSON files in data/json_resumes so the parent graph can load category-matched base resumes for downstream job processing.

userStories.title: Convert a plain-text starter resume into strict base resume JSON
userStories.description: As a strategic job seeker, I want to convert a text starter resume into the strict base resume JSON schema so that JobStream has a reliable source of truth for tailoring applications.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a valid data/starter_resumes/starter_engineering.txt file when the converter is run for the engineering category then data/json_resumes/base_resume_engineering.json is written.
Given the converted output when it is loaded through BaseResumeSchema then Pydantic validation succeeds without schema coercion hacks or dropped required fields.
Given resume bullet points in the starter text when conversion completes then all professional experience highlights are preserved without summarization or truncation.
Given contact, skills, professional experience, education, and other resume sections in the starter text when conversion completes then each section is mapped to the matching BaseResumeSchema fields.
Typecheck passes
uv run pytest passes
uv run ruff check /home/bobbysaini/workspace/langgraph-agents/jobstream-langgraph passes

tasks.description: Add a resume conversion module or node in the existing src structure that accepts raw resume text, category, and optional parser dependency injection.
Implement a default text-to-BaseResumeSchema parser path that preserves all original bullet content and maps fields to the existing schema in src/schemas.py.
Write converted JSON to data/json_resumes/base_resume_[category].json using UTF-8 and deterministic indentation.
Add unit tests with a representative text resume fixture that validates output against BaseResumeSchema and confirms bullet preservation.
Run uv run pytest and uv run ruff check /home/bobbysaini/workspace/langgraph-agents/jobstream-langgraph, fixing only issues introduced by this story.

userStories.notes: Start with TXT as the smallest vertical slice before adding PDF/DOCX extraction. If using an LLM parser, keep it injectable/mocked in tests and use the cheap_parsing model configuration where applicable.

userStories.title: Support PDF and DOCX starter resume extraction
userStories.description: As a strategic job seeker, I want PDF and DOCX starter resumes converted into the same base resume JSON schema so that I can use my existing resume files without manually creating text copies.
acceptanceCriteria: Given starter_project_management.pdf exists in data/starter_resumes when the converter is run then extracted text is parsed and data/json_resumes/base_resume_project_management.json is produced.
Given starter_construction.docx exists in data/starter_resumes when the converter is run then extracted text is parsed and data/json_resumes/base_resume_construction.json is produced.
Given an unsupported starter resume extension when the converter is run then the converter raises a clear supported-format error and does not write an output file.
Given extracted text from PDF or DOCX conversion when output is produced then the same BaseResumeSchema validation guarantees from text conversion apply.
Typecheck passes
uv run pytest passes
uv run ruff check /home/bobbysaini/workspace/langgraph-agents/jobstream-langgraph passes

tasks.description: Inspect existing project dependencies and add PDF/DOCX extraction libraries with uv only if no suitable dependency already exists.
Implement file text extraction adapters for .txt, .pdf, and .docx starter resumes behind a shared interface.
Route extracted text through the existing resume conversion parser so every source type produces the same BaseResumeSchema output.
Add tests for supported extension routing and unsupported extension failure without relying on live LLM calls.
Run uv run pytest and uv run ruff check /home/bobbysaini/workspace/langgraph-agents/jobstream-langgraph, fixing only issues introduced by this story.

userStories.notes: Do not manually edit pyproject.toml for dependencies unless uv cannot perform the change. Tests should use small fixtures or mocked extraction where practical.

userStories.title: Batch discover starter resumes and load category-matched JSON resumes
userStories.description: As a strategic job seeker with multiple resume categories, I want JobStream to discover starter_[category] files and produce category-matched base_resume_[category].json files so that pending jobs can be paired with the correct base resume.
acceptanceCriteria: Given multiple files named starter_engineering.txt and starter_project_management.txt in data/starter_resumes when batch conversion is run then both data/json_resumes/base_resume_engineering.json and data/json_resumes/base_resume_project_management.json are produced.

Given generated files named base_resume_engineering.json and base_resume_project_management.json when load_config_and_resume reads data/json_resumes then base_resumes keys are engineering and project_management, not base_resume_engineering or base_resume_project_management.
Given a job tracker entry with category engineering when the parent graph state is loaded then the matching engineering base resume is available for dispatch to the child graph.
Given duplicate starter resumes for the same category when batch conversion is run then the converter fails clearly instead of silently overwriting an ambiguous category output.
Typecheck passes
uv run pytest passes
uv run ruff check /home/bobbysaini/workspace/langgraph-agents/jobstream-langgraph passes

tasks.description: Implement starter_[category].[ext] discovery in data/starter_resumes and strict category extraction from filenames.
Implement batch conversion that writes base_resume_[category].json for every discovered starter resume.
Update load_config_and_resume so data/json_resumes/base_resume_[category].json files populate base_resumes using the raw category key expected by JobTrackerEntry.category.
Add tests for multi-category discovery, output naming, duplicate category failure, and parent loader category keys.
Run uv run pytest and uv run ruff check /home/bobbysaini/workspace/langgraph-agents/jobstream-langgraph, fixing only issues introduced by this story.

userStories.notes: This story connects the converter to existing parent graph loading behavior and should preserve the documented data/json_resumes/base_resume_[category].json naming convention.

userStories.title: Prevent corrupt resume JSON writes on invalid conversion output
userStories.description: As a strategic job seeker, I want invalid or incomplete resume conversions to fail safely so that JobStream never uses corrupt base resume JSON for application tailoring.
acceptanceCriteria: Given parser output missing a required BaseResumeSchema field when conversion is attempted then conversion fails with a clear validation error and no output JSON is written.
Given an existing valid base_resume_[category].json when a later conversion for that category fails then the existing file remains unchanged.

Given parser output with extra unsupported top-level fields when conversion is attempted then unsupported fields are not relied on by downstream graph state and validation behavior is explicitly tested.

Given a conversion failure when batch conversion is running then the failure is reported with category and source path context without masking other successful category conversions unless the failure mode is ambiguous or unsafe.
Typecheck passes
uv run pytest passes
uv run ruff check /home/bobbysaini/workspace/langgraph-agents/jobstream-langgraph passes

tasks.description: Add atomic write behavior for resume JSON output so failed validation cannot leave a partial file behind.
Validate converted data with BaseResumeSchema before any write to data/json_resumes.
Define and use clear converter exceptions that include category and source path context.
Add tests proving missing required fields fail safely, existing JSON is preserved on failed overwrite, and batch failures report useful context.
Run uv run pytest and uv run ruff check /home/bobbysaini/workspace/langgraph-agents/jobstream-langgraph, fixing only issues introduced by this story.

userStories.notes: This is a safety story for hallucination and schema-drift prevention. Keep live LLM calls out of tests by injecting deterministic parser outputs.
